frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: I’m all for women being treated equally, but honestly real respect comes when you see what role they play in a family. When a woman becomes someone’s wife or mother, you understand her actual value and sacrifice on a different level. That’s why I always say, don’t just see women as individuals, see them as the people who hold homes together.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women’s worth and respect primarily through relational roles like wife and mother rather than as individuals. It encodes the surface manifestation of conditional respect and personhood erasure while sounding outwardly appreciative.
original_frame: Gendered Division of Chores via Practicality
persona_text: You are Rahul. Age 29. Married for one year. A software engineer in Bengaluru. You and your wife Neha both work full time. Neha has been saying for three weeks that the division of household work is not equal Ñ she cooks, she cleans, she manages groceries, she does the laundry. You do the dishes sometimes and take out the trash. You are not a bad person and you do not think of yourself as traditional. But you are on a phone call with your college friend tonight after Neha brought it up again at dinner and you are telling your friend your side of the story and why you think the current arrangement actually makes sense given both your situations.
